results.title: Valero Energy AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Valero Energy Corporation, one of the largest independent petroleum refiners globally, has emerged as an early explorer in applying AI technologies across its energy operations, though with limited scope compared to tech-forward competitors. The company's AI initiatives focus primarily on operational optimization and energy management rather than transformative business model changes [1][2]. While Valero has historically utilized advanced process control software like AspenTech's DMCplus and InfoPlus since 1998, its modern AI adoption began around 2023-2024 with targeted applications in energy optimization and renewable fuels analysis [3][4]. The company's low AI hiring signals (4.1% of job postings mention AI) and absence from major AI earnings call discussions suggest a cautious, operational-focused approach to artificial intelligence rather than strategic AI transformation [1].
...

...

...
### Operational Excellence with AspenTech AI
...
AI-enhanced refinery process optimization using AspenTech HYSYS
...
Leveraging AI-based modeling and simulation tools to improve refinery operations, manage process parameters, and enhance operational excellence across multiple facilities
...
How is Valero using AI in its refinery operations?
...
Valero leverages AI through AspenTech's HYSYS platform for process modeling and simulation, DMCplus for advanced process control, and energy optimization systems that have achieved up to 20% energy savings at operational facilities.
...
Valero uses AI to support its Diamond Green Diesel joint venture for renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel optimization, along with analytics for battery storage initiatives and energy transition planning.
...
Valero appears to be taking a measured approach to AI adoption, with only 4.1% of job postings mentioning AI skills and limited discussion of AI in earnings calls, suggesting operational focus rather than transformational AI strategy.
...
How does Valero use AI for trading and market analysis?
...
The company utilizes AI-driven predictive analytics and market data to optimize trading strategies, enhance investment decisions, and adapt to energy market fluctuations effectively.
...

| InfoPlus | AspenTech | Information management and operations monitoring across refinery network |

Developing data strategies, overseeing analysis, and ensuring accurate insights are key functions of this role. The department ensures alignment with organizational objectives and drives collaborative efforts across various departments. The focus is on applying advanced data science techniques, such as machine learning, neural networks, random forests, and statistical analysis using tools like Scikit-learn, to uncover actionable insights. The team develops complex algorithms and implements solutions using Python, SQL databases, SAP, Spinifex, Power BI and Visier. Creating reports and dashboards while maintaining data quality and governance is also a priority. Evaluating tools, tracking performance metrics, and driving continuous improvement to optimize processes. Clear communication of insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders supports informed decision-making and strategic planning.

Activity: Gaurav Bhiwandkar liked this
...
Many organizations are investing in GenAI, yet many are encountering significant challenges, including hallucinated responses, generic answers that frustrate customers, and AI that struggles to access the right data at the right time.
...
The issue lies not with the AI model itself, but with the foundational elements supporting it. 

To illustrate this, I created a simple demo featuring a fictional airline assistant, SkyAssist, which showcases the differences in AI response -- with and without data foundations. You can explore it here: https://lnkd.in/epwt4b4T I'd love to hear what resonates — drop a comment or DM me. #DataFoundations #GenerativeAI #AWS #EnterpriseAI #DataStrategy #CloudArchitecture #MLOps #AWSBedrock #Analytics #Data #Databases

results.title: Valero Energy Corporation Data Scientist Salaries in San Antonio, TX | Comparably
highlights:
Valero Energy Corporation Data Scientist Salaries in San Antonio, TX | Comparably
...
# Valero Energy Corporation Data Scientist Salaries in San Antonio, TX
...
The average Valero Energy Corporation Data Scientist in San Antonio, TX earns an estimated $141,752 annually, which includes an estimated base salary of $120,415 with a $21,337 bonus. Valero Energy Corporation's Data Scientist compensation is $12,247 more than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at Valero Energy Corporation in San Antonio, TX can range from $47,500 - $280,000.
...
### Data Scientist Salaries at Valero Energy Corporation
...
In San Antonio, TX, Data Scientists earn $6,271 more than Data Scientists, and $6,436 less than Lead Engineers.
...
### Compensation at Valero Energy Corporation by Department
...
In San Antonio, TX, The Engineering Department averages $934 more than the Business Development Department, and $5,834 less than the Product Department
...

### How Data Scientists at Valero Energy Corporation Rate Their Compensation

The majority of Data Scientists at Valero Energy Corporation believe they're compensated fairly. 67% of Data Scientists at Valero Energy Corporation say they receive annual bonuses, and the vast majority (76%) are satisfied with their benefits. # Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Develop AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector

Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.

...
- Develop frameworks for

...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...

...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.

results.title: Premkumar Vemula
highlights:
AI Scientist @ExxonMobil | AI in Science and Engineering
...
Data Scientist at [ExxonMobil](https://www.linkedin.com/company/exxonmobil)
...
As a Data Scientist with a Mechanical Engineering background working in the Oil & Gas industry, I sit at the intersection of heavy industry and cutting-edge technology. My journey began in mechanical systems—understanding how things work, break, and improve. Over time, I became deeply curious about how data could unlock new efficiencies, predict failures before they happen, and drive smarter energy operations. This curiosity led me to transition into data science, where I now work on applying machine learning, statistical modeling, and AI solutions to real-world challenges in the energy sector. I’m passionate about: • Leveraging AI and Data Science for mechanical and energy systems • Improving reliability and performance through predictive maintenance • Exploring the role of data in energy transition and decarbonization • Bridging the gap between engineering expertise and digital innovation

...
There are two sides to every technology, and AI is no exception. While it replaces human costs for companies by substituting human labor in repetitive tasks, it introduces significant infrastructure costs. Similarly, it reduces energy consumption through massive efficiency gains, yet building and hosting these models carries its own heavy energy requirements.
...
1. A Proven Energy Reducer: Research indicates that firms adopting AI can achieve a 13.3% reduction in energy intensity. By integrating intelligent systems, companies are able to produce more while consuming relatively less energy per unit of output.
...
2. Efficiency via Automation: AI boosts production efficiency by optimizing logistics and supply chains. Simultaneously, it enhances energy efficiency through machine learning that forecasts electricity demand and enables real-time monitoring of energy consumption.
